About

I am a PhD student in Astrophysics at the Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA), Bangalore, India. My doctoral research focuses on the interstellar medium, with a particular emphasis on dust polarization and magnetic fields in high lattitude clouds.

Background

I completed my masters in Physics from National Institute of Technology Rourkela before joining IIA for my doctoral studies.

Research Interests

  • Structure and dynamics of the interstellar medium
  • Dust grain alignment theory and polarimetry
  • Magnetic field morphology in molecular clouds
  • Multi-wavelength observational astrophysics
  • Statistical methods for polarization analysis

Beyond Research

When I am not at the telescope or writing code, I enjoy reading non-fiction books, photography and exploring the intersections of science and philosophy.

Feel free to get in touch if you would like to discuss research, potential collaborations, or anything astrophysics-related.
